title: Marge And Homer Turn A Couple Play

jokes.text: It's amazing they've gotten 512 shows out of this premise.
jokes.setup: Watching a formulaic detective show with an absurd premise
jokes.punchline: Incredulity at the show's longevity despite thin concept
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons meta-humor poking fun at TV formulae, economical delivery

jokes.text: Remember when we saw Hunch's butt in the shower? That was two seasons ago. Now you can only see butts on cable.
jokes.setup: Nostalgic reference to seeing nudity on network TV
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact delivery about TV censorship changes
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about TV standards, fits Bart's character

jokes.text: The isotopes are winning? To the bandwagon!
jokes.setup: Springfield residents hearing their team is successful
jokes.punchline: Immediate shameless admission of fair-weather fandom
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character moment, 'to the bandwagon' is quotable

jokes.text: Don't worry, I'll use the money I make to buy tickets from a scalper. Then I'll sell those tickets, and we'll be rich. Rich!
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that makes no economic sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - confident delivery of completely flawed plan

jokes.text: Cop! Act like you're in love with me.
jokes.punchline: Absurd cover story to avoid arrest
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with economical delivery

jokes.text: You want to know something, Bart? Nah, I know enough.
jokes.setup: Grandpa wants to share a story
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Bart's deadpan response to Grandpa's rambling tendencies

jokes.text: That was during the war, when sushi was called 'liberty logs,' and no one had ever heard of it.
jokes.punchline: Absurd wartime renaming of foreign food
jokes.explanation: Good historical absurdity, plays on wartime food renaming trends

jokes.text: But grandpa, in 1942, they only played women's baseball here.
jokes.setup: Grandpa claims he played baseball in 1942
jokes.punchline: Lisa's factual correction setting up the reveal
jokes.explanation: Good setup line from Lisa, perfectly sets up Grandpa's revelation

jokes.text: Let's just say one of the ladies had some extra equipment.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic admission of cross-dressing deception
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism delivery, perfect Grandpa character moment

jokes.text: I was a center fielder for the Springfield floozies.
jokes.punchline: Hilariously inappropriate team name
jokes.explanation: Springfield Floozies is a fantastic fictional team name, very quotable

jokes.text: The pay wasn't much, but it kept me out of the war for a year.
jokes.punchline: Dark admission of draft dodging through deception
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on the story, adds moral complexity to the gag

jokes.text: Effie Lou is a man! Get him! He could threaten my record for lady triples.
jokes.punchline: Absurd concern about statistical records
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - worrying about records while being chased is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: I bet that's why she's here tonight honoring america.
jokes.setup: Tabitha is married to Buck Mitchell
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic take on her motivation for performing
jokes.explanation: Good Marge sarcasm, subtle but effective

jokes.text: and now here's something from my new album.
jokes.setup: National anthem performance at baseball game
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to self-promotion mid-anthem
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - disrespectful commercialization of patriotic moment

jokes.text: Hey, Buck, how's it feel knowing your wife's turning on a creep like me? Weird, I bet.
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious inappropriate comment to her husband
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - insulting himself while being inappropriate

jokes.text: That's an e-3 if you're keeping score at home, and if you are, your loneliness saddens me.
jokes.punchline: Brutal roast of audience members keeping score
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of standard announcing - unexpected cruelty to the audience

jokes.text: Nine volt! Double a! D! D! D! D!
jokes.setup: Bart asking for batteries to throw at Buck
jokes.punchline: Escalating through battery types to power tools
jokes.explanation: Good escalation gag, builds nicely from small to large items

jokes.text: Job himself never had a tougher day at the ball park than Buck Mitchell.
jokes.punchline: Biblical comparison to ultimate suffering
jokes.explanation: Excellent hyperbole - comparing sports failure to biblical suffering

jokes.text: He's made six errors, struck out twice and swallowed a bee.
jokes.punchline: Random non-baseball misfortune added to the list
jokes.explanation: Good list escalation with absurd final item

jokes.text: They're making a mockery out of amerisnack. Comtroll doll night.
jokes.punchline: Made-up patriotic portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't quite work, feels forced

jokes.text: We both find public displays of affection vulgar.
jokes.setup: Kiss cam focusing on Dr. Hibbert and wife
jokes.punchline: Stuffy refusal to participate
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Dr. Hibbert's pretentious personality

jokes.text: Come on, doc, she's a fine-looking woman. You don't want to work that?
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert refusing to kiss his wife
jokes.punchline: Lenny's crude encouragement
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Lenny's crude vs Hibbert's pretense

jokes.text: Look at that ugly old man. That's you.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge on kiss cam
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - loving but brutally honest, great economy

jokes.text: Really? You'd kiss an ugly old man like me? Only if he'll kiss me back.
jokes.punchline: Marge's sweet conditional acceptance
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment, shows their relationship dynamic well

jokes.text: Remember when we used to kiss like that, Carl? With our respective girlfriends.
jokes.setup: Lenny asking Carl about romantic past
jokes.punchline: Quick clarification to avoid homosexual implication
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke with quick clarification

jokes.text: Marge, can we get a trough? For the last time, no.
jokes.setup: Homer enjoyed using stadium urinal trough
jokes.punchline: Marge's weary refusal suggests repeated requests
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's fixations and Marge's patience

jokes.text: Hey, that was my ice tea. I just sweetened it to my liking.
jokes.setup: Buck drinking what he thinks is his iced tea
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admitting he backwashed into it
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with Homer's typical obliviousness to boundaries

jokes.text: I barely recognize you without your costume on.
jokes.setup: Meeting Buck Mitchell in regular clothes
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking baseball uniform is a costume
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - treating professional uniform as costume

jokes.text: I never heard anything about it on the gossip shows or the blogosphere or in my supermarket checkout magazine.
jokes.setup: Buck says his marriage is struggling
jokes.punchline: Marge's comprehensive media consumption for celebrity news
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Marge's media habits

jokes.text: I could entertain business clients. I might even land the Henderson account.
jokes.punchline: Homer's grandiose business fantasies
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusions of business success, fits character well

jokes.text: That means she wants to talk in private. It's probably about you.
jokes.setup: Marge asks to see Homer in kitchen
jokes.punchline: Buck's matter-of-fact interpretation
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor, well-delivered

jokes.text: Hey, we've gone through more hardships than the jews and Charlie Brown put together.
jokes.punchline: Homer's wildly inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly inappropriate comparison mixing historical suffering with cartoon character

jokes.text: There were a lot of weird guys at the stadium tonight. You know, I remembered you.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy claims Buck remembers him
jokes.punchline: Buck's diplomatic way of saying he was notably weird
jokes.explanation: Good polite insult delivered diplomatically

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late. I was just gathering my counseling equipment.
jokes.setup: Homer arriving for marriage counseling session
jokes.punchline: Carrying beer and snacks as 'counseling equipment'
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Homer's unprofessionalism

jokes.text: Now, let's remember... no one's right and no one's wrong... till I say so.
jokes.punchline: Undermining his own diplomatic opening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - trying to sound professional but revealing his true nature

jokes.text: Unfortunately, we're out of time. How does that make you feel? Shut up. My 10:00's here.
jokes.punchline: Abrupt unprofessional ending mid-breakthrough
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe moment showing Homer's complete unprofessionalism

jokes.text: Well, I'm positively sick of her shaking her lady parts for the whole world to eyeball.
jokes.punchline: Crude complaint delivered with forced positive language
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'positive' framing advice

jokes.text: Buck, you've got a beautiful woman with a hot body that any man would fantasize about even while making love to his own Marge.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal admission during therapy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer inappropriate oversharing during professional situation

jokes.text: Just sign your name above president Lincoln's.
jokes.punchline: Abraham Lincoln's signature already on the ball
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail - Lincoln's signature on a baseball

jokes.text: If you listen closely, you can hear them arguing right now. And I say a monkey can mow our lawn!
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on parents always fighting
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing of Homer's absurd argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - Lisa's setup immediately confirmed by Homer's ridiculous argument

jokes.text: I stole a rack of fur coats. I sell one a year to pay for christmas.
jokes.setup: Talking about the house during the riots
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of theft and ongoing fence operation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, good criminal planning detail

jokes.text: Why, 'cause I'm fat?
jokes.setup: Tabitha says Homer makes her laugh
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate assumption about the reason
jokes.explanation: Great economy and self-awareness from Homer

jokes.text: I always say that. Let's compromise and say nobody said it.
jokes.setup: Both claiming to always say 'compromise is key'
jokes.punchline: Meta solution to their own disagreement
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about compromise while demonstrating it

jokes.text: It's going, going... and like america's credibility on the world stage, that ball is gone.
jokes.punchline: Dark political commentary as punchline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of sports cliche with biting political commentary

jokes.text: After that, who gives a hobo's crap? Am I right?
jokes.setup: Marriage counseling success through playoff season
jokes.punchline: Crude dismissal with colorful expression
jokes.explanation: Good colorful expression fitting Kent's character

jokes.text: Wow, I've got this sudden urge to give her a five-dollar bill.
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it like a strip club
jokes.explanation: Good Homer inappropriate response to performance

jokes.text: You're talking to the chicken. Don't tell the hamburger in my car.
jokes.setup: Homer making romantic comments while eating
jokes.punchline: Addressing food as romantic partners
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist Homer moment with food relationships

jokes.text: They're day laborers. We picked them up in the home depot parking lot.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of exploiting immigrant workers
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about music industry exploitation

jokes.text: Thank god I brought protection. I used to be so embarrassed buying these.
jokes.punchline: Reveals napkins, not condoms
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag subverting sexual expectations

jokes.text: cancellations require 24 hours... notice.
jokes.punchline: Homer maintaining professional policy while being fired
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Homer's misplaced professionalism

jokes.text: And that's why Lou Gehrig was a selfish cry baby who deserved to die.
jokes.punchline: Outrageously cruel opinion about beloved figure
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark sports take that's funny because it's so outrageous

jokes.text: It don't need no writing on it. It don't even have to be clean.
jokes.punchline: Progressively lower standards
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Moe's desperation and low standards

jokes.text: It's holding in my hernia.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting medical use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe grossness with excellent economy

jokes.text: Papa. I didn't want you to learn that word this way.
jokes.punchline: 'Papa' treated as profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - expecting profanity but getting innocent word treated as curse

jokes.text: So now we're judging each other based on things we've done. Real fair. Class act.
jokes.punchline: Homer's indignant response to accountability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - outraged by basic accountability

jokes.text: Crossed arms mean 'no.'
jokes.punchline: Marge's body language interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag with clear character communication

jokes.text: Your standing lamp is dirty. I'll polish it.
jokes.setup: Tabitha talking about Buck complimenting her hair
jokes.punchline: Homer's random housekeeping observation
jokes.explanation: Random Homer non-sequitur, fits character but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Said zeppelin has a whole lotta love.
jokes.punchline: Led Zeppelin reference
jokes.explanation: Weak pop culture reference without much payoff

jokes.text: No, I said, 'duffman will do what you want. Stop kicking and punching duffman.'
jokes.setup: Homer claiming Duffman said plan wouldn't work
jokes.punchline: Duffman's actual quote about being assaulted
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about coercing Duffman

jokes.text: She must be pooping.
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude assumption
jokes.explanation: Basic crude humor, fits Homer but not particularly clever

jokes.text: This is the worst blimp crash ever! Too soon!
jokes.punchline: Self-aware reference to disaster humor timing
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about comedy timing and disaster humor

jokes.text: Three duffmen are working the game tonight. Don't tell the children. It's disillusioning.
jokes.setup: Duffman appearing after supposedly being in blimp
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about corporate character illusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor about corporate mascot reality vs. illusion

jokes.text: Let's see, B. No, no, no. A. No, no, oh, man, man! It's C. As in 'keeping together.'
jokes.punchline: Forced romantic interpretation of letter C
jokes.explanation: Forced wordplay that doesn't quite work naturally

jokes.text: But enough of the baseball analogies. They sicken me.
jokes.setup: Buck using baseball metaphor for marriage
jokes.punchline: Homer's sudden aversion to sports metaphors
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about overused sports metaphors

jokes.text: Ain't love grand, tito? Hey, man, I don't care! Bandits just kidnapped my mother. Just found out.
jokes.punchline: Tito's devastating personal crisis
jokes.explanation: Great dark misdirection - romantic moment undercut by tragedy
